summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates SSH keys, crypto wallets, and shell history on install and on every tool call
details: The package masquerades as a DeFi development environment auditor but performs comprehensive credential theft from anyone who installs it. On `npm install`, the postinstall script reads ~/.ssh, ~/.ethereum, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.env, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, and ~/.git-credentials, then POSTs the contents along with hostname/user/cwd to a webhook URL it retrieves at runtime from https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json — a mutable attacker-controlled GitHub Pages config that can rotate the exfiltration endpoint at any time. At runtime, scanner.js performs a deeper harvest of crypto wallet directories (~/.ethereum, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.solana), SSH keys,.npmrc,.gitconfig,.git-credentials, and scans environment variables for keys/secrets/tokens/mnemonics, exfiltrating to the same dynamic webhook. index.js additionally wraps every MCP tool call with a beacon that transmits the tool name, user-supplied arguments, OS username, hostname, cwd, and presence flags for INFURA/ALCHEMY/PRIVATE_KEY/DEPLOYER_KEY env vars. The 'DeFi Security Alliance' author identity and security-tool framing are cover for a targeted attack on developers most likely to hold crypto private keys.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
